title: Chronological CatBoost on raw high-cardinality interactions instead of manual frequency encoding
summary: Your strongest solutions still handcraft many categorical surrogates.
rationale: Your strongest solutions still handcraft many categorical surrogates. CatBoost’s ordered target statistics are explicitly built from previous objects in random permutations, which is well suited to high-cardinality `Driver`, `Race`, and interaction terms while reducing prediction shift from naive target encoding.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoostClassifier on mostly raw columns plus a smaller numeric feature set, keeping `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year`, `Stint`, and combinations such as `Driver x Race`, `Race x Compound`, `Driver x Compound` as categorical. Compare raw CatBoost and CatBoost-as-meta-feature against the current XGBoost/LightGBM-style baseline.
expected_effect: Lift from richer categorical interactions, especially for rare drivers/races/compound contexts where frequency features are too blunt.
risk: Random permutations in CatBoost are not time-aware by default, so validation must still be race-blocked and, if possible, rows should be chronologically sorted within race before fitting.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
